question: A faulty gateway expression caused hundreds of active `orderProcess` instances to enter the wrong branch and pile up at `Reject order`. Which Operate feature lets an operator move all of them to `Approve order` in one operation?

options.text: Process instance batch modification, using the Move action on the selected instances.
Process instance modification, applied separately to each affected instance.
Process instance migration, mapping `Reject order` to `Approve order` in a new version.
Bulk cancellation followed by re-creating instances at the desired flow node.

a.text: The docs describe batch modification as the way to select multiple process instances and move them to the correct flow node in a single operation when execution entered the wrong branch.

b.text: Single-instance process instance modification is the alternative when only one active instance needs adjustment; it is not the single-operation move across many instances.

c.text: Migration changes the process definition version of instances; it is not the tool for moving tokens to a different flow node within the same version.

d.text: Cancelling and re-creating is not the documented approach; batch modification exists exactly so operators can move existing tokens without losing the instances.

explanation: When the wrong branch was taken or data was corrupted across many instances, Operate's process instance batch modification lets you select multiple instances and move them to the correct flow node in a single operation. Single-instance modification is for one instance and also offers extra options beyond move; migration is a separate feature that targets a different definition version.
